heading: Exemptions
label: 4103 Exemptions
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource section (U.S. Code)410346:525hSection 4103 contains an exemption from carrying fire extinguishers, for on a vessel competing in an organized race or a vessel designed and intended for racing only.
Amendments1988— added subsec. (a) and designated existing provisions as subsec. (b).Pub. L. 100–540
amendments: Amendments1988— added subsec. (a) and designated existing provisions as subsec. (b).Pub. L. 100–540

children.text: good cause exists for granting an exemption; and
the safety of the vessel and individuals on board will not be adversely affected.

children.label: b
children.text: does not apply to a vessel propelled by outboard motors when competing in a race previously arranged and announced or, if the vessel is designed and intended only for racing, when operated incidental to tuning up the vessel and its engines for the race.Section 4102(a) of this title
